title: Using GCF to Solve a Grouping Problem

steps.instruction: You have 30 pencils and 45 erasers. You want to make identical gift bags with no items left over. What is the greatest number of bags you can make?
steps.work: We need the largest number that divides evenly into both 30 and 45 — the GCF.
steps.explanation: The GCF tells us the maximum number of equal groups where both quantities are divided evenly.
Take the lowest power of each common prime factor and multiply them.

steps.instruction: How many bags, and what goes in each?
steps.work: 15 bags. Each bag gets 30 ÷ 15 = 2 pencils and 45 ÷ 15 = 3 erasers.
steps.explanation: The GCF (15) is the number of bags. Dividing each total by 15 tells us the contents per bag.

title: Using LCM to Find When Events Coincide

steps.instruction: Mia waters her plants every 6 days and feeds them every 10 days. She did both today. In how many days will she do both again?
steps.work: We need the smallest number that is a multiple of both 6 and 10 — the LCM.
steps.explanation: The LCM tells us when two repeating cycles will align again.
Take the highest power of each prime factor that appears in either number.

steps.work: Mia will water and feed on the same day again in 30 days.
steps.explanation: 30 is divisible by both 6 (30 ÷ 6 = 5) and 10 (30 ÷ 10 = 3), and no smaller number works.
